title: Live gaming apparatus with dice shaker and gaming wheel
abstract: The present disclosure relates to systems and methods for live gaming with dice and roulette systems. Various embodiments may include a gaming apparatus comprising a dice shaker and a gaming wheel providing a plurality of outcomes. Examples may be configured to activate the dice shaker to generate a roll outcome and activate the gaming wheel based on the roll outcome. The results of the gaming wheel may apply a multiplier to the gaming event. The dice shaker and gaming wheel elements may include physical and/or virtual elements associated with the gaming apparatus.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A gaining system, comprising: a dice shaker configured to roll at least one die; a gaming wheel comprising a face displaying a pl urality of gaming outcomes; a gaming apparatus in communication with the dice shaker and the gaming wheel; and a processor and at least one memory comprising instructions, which when executed by the processor, causes the gaming system to: in response to player input received at the gaming apparatus, activate the dice shaker to roll the at least one die and generate a roll outcome; activate the gaming wheel using a movement characteristic based on the roll outcome; and determine a randomized outcome of the gaming wheel. 2. The gaming system of claim 1 , wherein the at least one memory comprises instructions, which when executed by the processor, causes the system to apply a multiplier to a gaming event at the gaming apparatus, the multiplier corresponding to the randomized outcome associated with the gaming wheel. 3. The gaming system of claim 1. further comprising: a controller associated with the gaming apparatus, wherein the controller determines at least one of a timing and a force of the roll at the dice shaker. 4. The gaming system of claim 3, wherein the controller is a button. 5. The gaming system of claim 3. wherein the controller measures an activation force, and wherein the activation force determines the force of the roll. 6. The gaming system of claim 1. wherein the gaming wheel is a standard roulette wheel. 7. The gaming system of claim 6, wherein the standard roulette wheel spins at a constant speed to maintain randomness, and the constant speed corresponds to a roulette ball launch speed. 8. The gaming system of claim 7. wherein standard roulette wheel spins during a launch of the roulette ball, and at least until the roulette ball comes to rest. 9. The gaming system of claim 1, wherein the gaming apparatus further comprises a display displaying at least one of the roll outcome, and a gaming outcome. 10. The gaming system of claim 1, wherein the gaming apparatus is an individual player machine comprising a base section supporting the dice shaker and the gaming wheel. 11. The gaming system of claim 1. wherein the dice shaker and the gaming wheel are virtual, and wherein the dice shaker and the gaming wheel are provided on a display associated with the gaming apparatus. 12. The gaming system of claim 1. wherein the gaming wheel comprises a plurality of pockets, and wherein each pocket is associated with a gaming outcome. 13. The gaming system of claim 12, wherein the at least one memory comprises instructions, which when executed by the processor, causes the system to: launch a ball into the gaming wheel at a speed based on the roll outcome, wherein the gaming wheel spins to maintain randomness; and determine, by at least one sensor, a pocket in which the ball comes to rest, wherein the pocket determines the multiplier. 14. The gaming system of claim 13, wherein the sensor is at least one of an image sensor, a proximity' sensor, a light sensor, a pressure sensor, and a radio frequency identification (RFID) sensor. 15. A method for gaming, comprising: receiving, at a gaming apparatus, instructions to initiate a gaming event; activating a dice shaker configured to roll at least one die and generate a roll outcome, wherein the dice shaker is associated with the gaming apparatus; 

 activating a gaming wheel using a movement characteristic based on the roll outcome, wherein the movement characteristic corresponds to at least one of a wheel spin and a ball launch; and determining a randomized outcome of the gaming wheel. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein activating the dice shaker automatically occurs during the gaming event. 17. The method of claim 15, wherein activating the dice shaker occurs in response to receiving player input at the gaming apparatus. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the player input is received via a controller associated with the gaming apparatus. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising: determining at least one of a timing and a force of the dice roll based on the player input. 20. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium comprising instructions stored thereon that cause a computing device to at least: receive, at a gaming apparatus, instructions to initiate a gaming event; activate a dice shaker configured to roll at least one die and generate a roll outcome, wherein the dice shaker is associated with the gaming apparatus; activate a gaming wheel using a movement characteristic based on the roll outcome, wherein the movement characteristic corresponds to at least one of a spin and a ball launch; and determine a randomized outcome of the gaming wheel.
